text:
<@UCUSW7WVD> the `[+]` I believe means that the editor buffer is changed. You can only see it when I am in insert mode because the video covers it otherwise. The tool only knows about the file, it polls for modifications, evaluates it and writes back the re-stringified AST.

I guess for presenting this project, I should maybe use a more mainstream editor, because the terminal view does potentially alienate those not familiar with e.g. vim. I also need to find a way to communicate better when exactly the file is saved, it's a bit hard to tell in the video because I just type the command very fast.

text:
<@UCUSW7WVD> I just noticed I misread one of your messages above, you were asking about the repo for the text, not the code?

It's here: <https://git.s-ol.nu/mmm/blob/master/root/research/alivecoding/text%24markdown+sidenotes.md?markup|https://git.s-ol.nu/mmm/blob/master/root/research/alivecoding/text%24markdown+sidenotes.md?markup>

There's also a PoC clientside editor built into my CMS (<https://mmm.s-ol.nu/research/alivecoding/:%20text/html+interactive|https://mmm.s-ol.nu/research/alivecoding/:%20text/html+interactive>) but you wont be able to save changes and performance is probably meh

text: <@UCUSW7WVD> it just polls the file modification time with stat(2). I could implement proper support for inotify, FSEvents etc, but those APIs are all platform specific and the benefit is marginal
<@UCUSW7WVD> it just polls the file modification time with stat(2). I could implement proper support for inotify, FSEvents etc, but those APIs are all platform specific and the benefit is marginal
Yeah. I don't care about performance. Just thinking about how this would work reliably without losing what's typed in. Is the Kakoune integration also in your repo?

<https://github.com/hediet/vscode-debug-visualizer/tree/master/extension>
>  A VS Code extension for visualizing data structures while debugging. Works best with JavaScript/TypeScript. Also tested with C#, Java and PHP. Works with any language that you can debug in VS Code.

text:
<https://gist.github.com/luque/97235d8cf7d36cce02f47e24af441493>

> the  reality is that a usual software project stack involves an increasingly larger number of programming   languages, DSLs, frameworks, systems, tools, techniques and processes, so it is a fact that the   accidental complexity in our day-to-day software projects is increasing to unbearable levels.
> We plan to build a new breed of dynamic and fully conceptual modeling environment in order to enable  programmers to work through every development stage --analysis, specification, design,   implementation, deployment, evolution, etc.-- at the conceptual level and explore their dynamic   models as a thinking and learning tool.
h/t <https://twitter.com/rafael_luque/status/1232703579208765440>

attachments.fallback: <https://twitter.com/rafael_luque|@rafael_luque>: Some old thoughts about the need for a new way of thinking about software. <https://gist.github.com/luque/97235d8cf7d36cce02f47e24af441493> <https://twitter.com/miltonlopez_|@miltonlopez_> <https://twitter.com/HernanWilkinson|@HernanWilkinson> <https://twitter.com/girba|@girba> Any comments/suggestions from you will be very valuable.
attachments.text: Some old thoughts about the need for a new way of thinking about software. <https://gist.github.com/luque/97235d8cf7d36cce02f47e24af441493> <https://twitter.com/miltonlopez_|@miltonlopez_> <https://twitter.com/HernanWilkinson|@HernanWilkinson> <https://twitter.com/girba|@girba> Any comments/suggestions from you will be very valuable.

elements.text: the  reality is that a usual software project stack involves an increasingly larger number of programming   languages, DSLs, frameworks, systems, tools, techniques and processes, so it is a fact that the   accidental complexity in our day-to-day software projects is increasing to unbearable levels.

We plan to build a new breed of dynamic and fully conceptual modeling environment in order to enable  programmers to work through every development stage --analysis, specification, design,   implementation, deployment, evolution, etc.-- at the conceptual level and explore their dynamic   models as a thinking and learning tool.
h/t

text: The most natural flow may be: anytime a file is saved, it gets written, sent to `init.moon`, reread and the buffer refreshed. If this happened fast there'd be no opportunity for merge conflicts. Is this what you're already doing?

init.moon
, reread and the buffer refreshed. If this happened fast there'd be no opportunity for merge conflicts. Is this what you're already doing?
